hero.heading:
The name Tarrant
carries lineage.
hero.body: It is chosen carefully, because everything here is.

childhood.heading: The Maker
paragraphs: Katrina Phillips — The Tarrant Collective began where most considered things do — with questions that had not yet found their answers.
From her earliest beginnings, Katrina Phillips has been drawn to objects with intention and story.

Materials that, when understood and listened to, reveal a quiet honesty of their own. The cool, smooth weight of a stone absentmindedly rolling in the palm of her hand. The natural patina of leather earned through movement and time. The interrupted grain of raw linen. The enduring surface of worked brass and other alloys. The layered stories of polished timber. Each material reveals itself to the studied and attuned.

The intention and patience to learn became a practice, and the practice became The Tarrant. A precision carrying system built around the needs of a working designer. Every pocket, every panel considered. Nothing added without reason.
The name Tarrant belongs to a strong female lineage. Honoured. Strong. Unmoving. It is carried forward here with the same intention.

selfTaught.heading:
Self-taught.
Material-led.
paragraphs: Self-taught across painting, moulding and leatherwork, everything made under the Tarrant name starts the same way: with curiosity about what a material could become in the hands.
The Tarrant Collective is a studio, not just a product. The site architecture allows for future products across different categories and materials — not limited to leather.
